"One Call" is Gunna's first solo offering of 2019 and the lead single to Drip or Drown 2. On the track, he raps about his wealth and "drip," boasting his expensive clothing and jewelry.
Gunna first announced the track with a simple post on January 28, 2019. Two days later, he posted a snippet of the song.

About
Song Bio3 contributors

On "how you did that," both Gunna and Kodak Black speak from the perspective of women admiring them, answering hypothetical questions of how they accumulated their wealth and status that makes them attractive to potential partners.
The track, backed by a flute and piano instrumental, was first teased via an Instagram story on December 12, 2021.

Q&A
Find answers to frequently asked questions about the song and explore its deeper meaning
Ask a question
What did Gunna say about "‎how you did that"?
Genius Answer 1 contributor
No. "how you did that" marks the first collaboration between Gunna and Kodack Black.
How did this track perform on the charts?
Genius Answer 2 contributors
"how you did that" peaked at #47 on the Billboard Hot 100 on the week of January 22, 2022.
Has this song received any certifications?
Genius Answer 1 contributor
"how you did that" was certified Gold by the RIAA on April 1, 2025.

About
Song Bio2 contributors

Lil Duke and NAV reunite on "Pots N Pans," a song that details their drug usage, hence the title since some drugs are cooked using pots and pans. Continuing their respective lavish lifestyle talk, the two drop references to their cars, girls, and jewelry.

On August 5, 2020, Lil Duke confirmed that he had received NAV's vocals for a new collaboration. Then, on December 19, 2020, they were spotted filming the official music video for this song, which is where the snippet was played.

Have Lil Duke and NAV collaborated before?
Genius Answer 1 contributor
Yes, Lil Duke was featured on NAV's May 2020 song, "Free Santana," which is a part of the latter's commercial mixtape, Brown Boy 2.

text: "bread & butter" marks Gunna's first release following his December 2022 release after he plead guilty to one count of racketeering charges.

album: Gunna: A Gift & A Curse Setlist
text: Throughout the track, Gunna speaks on several people in the rap industry such as Young Thug, Lil Durk, Lil Baby, and Pierre "P" Thomas, the founder of Quality Control Music.

album: Gunna: A Gift & A Curse Setlist
text: Gunna tackled the public response following his taking a plea deal in the YSL RICO case where several rappers decided to distance themselves from the Atlanta rapper in order to maintain their solidity towards Young Thug and YSL.

album: Gunna: A Gift & A Curse Setlist
text: Rapping in a slow and smooth cadence over a guitar beat, Gunna also claimed he was tricked by the district attorney into taking the plea and that it was by mistake.

About
Song Bio1 contributor

"Diamonds" is yet another track from frequent collaborators Young Thug and Gunna, with the two speaking and flexing about the expensive jewelry they own. This is the fourth track the two appear together in on the deluxe version of the record, with the first three prior being "Hot," "Surf," and the "Hot (Remix)."
Gunna was teased as a feature on a track of the deluxe version in the initial reveal of the record's updated edition, with Thug captioning his Instagram post:
«Deluxe out Friday... FT.

The subject of the 16th track of So Much Fun is suspected to be the ATL rapper YFN Lucci, with whom Young Thug has an ongoing feud.
Their beef goes back to 2017 when Thugger dubbed himself the new 2Pac after dropping Beautiful Thugger Girls. Lucci chimed in with his thoughts about Thug's statement tweeting:
«Pac would've never wore a dress
YFN Lucci is referring to Thug's fashion style, something that is often discussed in the hip-hop community, with some criticizing it and others loving it.
Talk about Young Thug's fashion choices started to grow a lot after Thug's album JEFFERY saw him in a dress for the album cover. In an interview with No Jumper Thug said:
«Nigga I wore this long ass dress because I had a motherfucking AK-47 up under it.
